codeReview.summary: The strict-schema normalization changes and multi-provider routing regressions are directionally correct, but the feature does not actually wire structured-output schemas through the core runner/provider path, so the runtime capability described by the feature and VAL-CORE-006 is still omitted.

issues.description: `get_output_schema` still hardcodes `None`, so the runner never carries an output schema into model calls. That means the new `AgentOutputSchema`/strict-validation helper behavior remains unreachable from the runtime: `Agent` still has no output-schema field (`crates/agents-core/src/agent.rs:279`), `ModelRequest` still has no schema/response-format slot (`crates/agents-core/src/models/interface.rs:35`), and the OpenAI payload builders only serialize input/tools/settings without any `response_format`/`json_schema` channel (`crates/agents-openai/src/models.rs:153`, `crates/agents-openai/src/models.rs:208`). The commit therefore improves helper coverage but does not deliver the structured-output runtime capability promised by the feature description or VAL-CORE-006.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `runtime-parity-worker` skill should explicitly say that a parity feature may legitimately include coverage-only closures for already-correct behavior, and how to document that without appearing to violate TDD.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/c8045f72-1cc6-4f08-ad3c-681b35cde0a4/handoffs/2026-04-05T13-47-07-374Z__implement-core-structured-output-and-provider-parity__9495a32b-909c-421b-abdd-bbfa547ee3c2.json -> handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false, with the step 4-5 deviation and suggestedChanges calling for this clarification.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not record that structured-output support is currently helper-only in Rust and is not yet threaded through `Agent`/runner/model requests, which makes structured-output parity tasks easy to under-scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md describes the core execution flow but not this limitation; current code shows `crates/agents-core/src/internal/turn_preparation.rs:40` returning `None` for output schema and `crates/agents-core/src/models/interface.rs:35-43` exposing no output-schema field on `ModelRequest`.

summary: Review failed: the commit tightens strict-schema helpers and provider-routing tests, but it still leaves structured-output schemas disconnected from the runtime request path, so the assigned parity feature is not fully implemented.
